description_long: This object consists of three single-story residential houses arranged side by side, each with a rectangular footprint and a prominent gable roof. The houses feature brick or stone-textured exterior walls and light gray, shingled roofs with subtle overhangs. Each house has a central entryway, with the middle house displaying a slightly larger and more pronounced front porch. Windows are distributed symmetrically on the front and sides, with dark window frames contrasting against the lighter wall surfaces. A small, detached fence or railing segment is positioned near the leftmost house, suggesting a yard or boundary feature. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of suburban or neighborhood settings.
description: A set of three single-story suburban houses with gable roofs, brick-textured walls, and symmetrical window arrangements, accompanied by a small detached fence.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the three houses in a row, highlighting the roof shapes and the detached fence to the side.
description_view_1: Frontal view displaying the main entryways, porches, and symmetrical window placements of each house, with the fence segment visible in front of the leftmost house.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back sides of the houses and the roof overhangs, with the fence segment positioned to the left.
description_view_3: Opposite frontal view showing the back facades and window placements, with clear visibility of the house arrangement.
attribute_entryway_design: Central front door with a small porch or overhang, simple steps, and minimal decorative elements.
